attacking_style: Pure sprinter who does not initiate attacks but excels in chaotic, disorganized finales where positioning and raw acceleration substitute for leadout trains. Thrives in the final 200-700m.
team_tactics: Does not rely heavily on organized leadout trains; DSM appears flexible, willing to pivot when circumstances change. Kooij operates effectively as opportunistic finisher when team support collapses.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint finales with technical or chaotic closing kilometers that disrupt rival leadout trains. Survives echelon conditions well enough to contest windswept Classics-style races.
vulnerability: Illness susceptibility noted at 2022 Giro forced complete withdrawal from sprint contention. Reliance on chaos may fail in perfectly organized sprint trains where positioning is locked early.
key_patterns: Exceptional late-race acceleration: gained 13 positions in final 200m when leadout failed (2 observations)
Thrives in disorganized sprints: won from confusion, nearly beat Philipsen from echelon (2 observations)
Mental resilience: bounced back next day after disappointment to win at 21 years old
Evolving from stage race sprinter to Classics contender: survived Classics-caliber echelon racing (2023 upgrade noted)
form_trajectory: positive: Clear progression from stage race opportunist (2022) to Classics-capable sprinter surviving Philipsen echelons and winning chaotic finales at 21 (early 2023)
